channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Nebraska court allows voter registrations from people with felony convictions
item.description: The Nebraska Supreme Court rejected the contention that the state’s pardon board has sole authority to restore such rights.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Harris interview with Fox News showcases a change in strategy for Democrats with network
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Kamala Harris ' interview with Fox News Channel's Bret Baier on Wednesday is the latest indication that Democrats during this campaign are increasingly willing to engage with a network well-stocked with supporters of opponent Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump focuses on women’s issues in front of all-women audience in Fox News town hall
item.description: The former president took questions on immigration, the economy and abortion in the hour-long town hall in front of a friendly crowd of women in suburban Atlanta.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben, MPR News

item.title: Judge in Trump election case directs prosecutors to turn over info related to Pence documents probe
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal judge overseeing the election interference case against Donald Trump directed prosecutors Wednesday to search for and provide to the former president's lawyers any Justice Department information related to a separate investigation into Mike Pence's handling of classifie
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris and Trump offer new details about policies and strategy in dueling interviews
item.description: As the presidential race moves into its final weeks, Democrat Kamala Harris and Republican Donald Trump have embarked on an interview blitz that has offered new details about their policy priorities and their political strategies.
item.dc:creator: Steve Peoples, Colleen Long And Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: North Carolina prepares to launch in-person early voting with mountain areas still recovering from Helene
item.description: North Carolina election officials are scrambling to expand their early voting programs to address fallout from Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Liz Cheney jumps into House races — for Democrats
item.description: Former Rep. Liz Cheney (R-Wyo.) is taking her anti-Trump crusade to the congressional battlefield, endorsing Democrats in a pair of contested House races in a sign that she sees the lower chamber as a potential check on former President Trump if he wins a second term in the White House.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk commits $70 million to boost Donald Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk, a tech mogul who is the world's richest person, plunged more than $70 million into helping Donald Trump and other Republicans win in November's election, making him one of the biggest donors to GOP causes this campaign season, according to campaign finance disclosures re
item.dc:creator: BRIAN SLODYSKO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump would be the oldest person to become president. He's not sharing health details
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — If he wins next month's election, Donald Trump would be the oldest person in U.S. history to be elected president. Yet the 78-year-old Republican nominee refuses to disclose new details about his physical or mental well-being, breaking decades of precedent.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES and LAURAN NEERGAARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Laken Riley Act sponsor blasts Bill Clinton's claim about Georgia student's death
item.description: Sen. Katie Britt criticized former President Bill Clinton for bringing up Laken Riley and noting that her death may have been prevented by proper border vetting.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nebraska court allows voter registrations from people with felony convictions
item.description: Nebraskans with felony convictions may register to vote after the state's highest court ruled Wednesday that a top election official had no authority to deem a law restoring those rights unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Chinese investments, rebukes for ‘preaching’ take center stage at contentious Casey v. McCormick rematch
item.description: Longtime Democratic Pennsylvania Sen. Bob Casey and Republican challenger Dave McCormick squared off in their second Senate debate ahead of Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris holds 24-point lead over Trump in California
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris continues to enjoy a wide lead over former president Donald Trump in her home state of California, according to the latest Emerson College Polling/Inside California Politics/The Hill poll released on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans grapple with storm of misinformation from their own party
item.description: Republicans representing states hit by hurricanes Helene and Milton are grappling with the spread of storm misinformation, much of which has stemmed from prominent figures in their own party — including sitting lawmakers and former President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hurricane fallout threatens to hinder voting
item.description: The fallout from a pair of deadly hurricanes threatens to roil voting in the Southeast with complications for election officials and residents alike in states key to this year’s presidential race.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump: Black and Hispanic voters who vote for Harris 'gotta have your head examined'
item.description: The former president suggested any Black or Hispanic voters who do not support him should have their head examined, echoing a similar comment he has made about Jewish voters backing Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Control of the Minnesota House is up for grabs this fall. Here are 14 races to watch
item.description: While all seats in the Minnesota House of Representatives are on the ballot this fall, just a fraction are taking up the focus and much of the fundraising for the major parties.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Georgia judge blocks ballot hand-count rule from taking effect before election
item.description: The rule was set to go into effect just two weeks before the election, and after early voting in the state is underway.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: ISIS-K orchestrated foiled Election Day terror plot: US official
item.description: Afghan native Nasir Ahmad Tawhedi, 27, was arrested in early October and accused of planning to target people on Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Trump uses interview on economics to promote tariffs and riff on his favorite themes
item.description: Donald Trump has seized on an opening to sound his frequent argument that imposing huge tariffs on foreign goods would amount to an economic elixir — one he claims would raise enormous sums for the government, protect U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Tillis warns conservatives’ 'unwise' reforms would 'weaken' Senate GOP
item.description: North Carolina Sen. Thom Tillis (R) circulated a “Dear Colleague” letter Tuesday warning that some of the reforms being pushed by Senate conservatives would only weaken the next Senate Republican leader and cause the same chaos that is plaguing the House Republican majority.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Trump town hall ends with extended 'musical-fest' while he stands onstage
item.description: At a town hall in Oaks, Pa., on Monday, Trump ended the Q&A portion to ask the audience to remain and listen to music with him.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben, MPR News

item.title: Harris to host Detroit town hall in bid to reach Black voters
item.description: The vice president will visit with Black entrepreneurs before the town hall with Charlamagne tha God.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada DMV fast-tracks credentials for potential voters
item.description: The Nevada Department of Motor Vehicles is seeking to move potential voters to the front of the line starting next week and is reminding new residents they can surrender their out-of-state ID.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump edges out Harris with early voters in battleground states
item.description: Former President Trump has a slight edge over Vice President Harris with early voters in battleground states, new polling shows, a promising sign for the Republican in the highly competitive presidential race.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: One-on-one with Mississippi's candidates in 2024 US Senate race
item.description: JACKSON, Miss. (WJTV) - U.S. Senator Roger Wicker (R-Miss.) and his Democratic opponent, Ty Pinkins, spoke to WJTV 12 News ahead of the November General Election. Wicker, a Pontotoc native, has been serving Mississippi in the U.S. Senate since 2007.
item.dc:creator: Tom Wright, NewsNation

item.title: 3 voter-related lawsuits dropped in Nevada ahead of Election Day
item.description: Petitioners who filed three lawsuits challenging the maintenance of Nevada’s voter rolls have dismissed their cases, in part because voting by-mail ballot was already well underway.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Native Voters could swing Arizona. Both parties want their votes.
item.description: Both Republicans and Democrats are trying to marshal Native American voters in Arizona, which could prove decisive to winning the key state.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Will Donald Trump and Kamala Harris debate again?
item.description: While there was originally talk of more than one meeting between Trump and Harris, the former president made his intentions clear with a post on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: Eric Adams is 'working well through the chaos': Gov. Hochul
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Gov. Kathy Hochul offered support for embattled New York City Mayor Eric Adams during the Columbus Day Parade in Manhattan on Monday. "Told him to bring in new blood… he's doing that… working well through the chaos," she told PIX11 News.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Control of state House could hinge on a sleepy district outside Duluth
item.description: Control of the Minnesota House of Representatives could come down to one race in a district outside Duluth, where first-term Republican Natalie Zeleznikar is being challenged by Democratic retired judge Mark Munger.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: Bob Woodward takes NPR behind the headline-grabbing moments in his new book
item.description: Bob Woodward speaks to NPR about the revelations in his new book, and recounts how key moments and meetings in recent years played out behind closed doors.
item.dc:creator: Scott Detrow, Courtney Dorning, and Avery Keatley, MPR News

item.title: Man with loaded gun arrested at checkpoint near Donald Trump's weekend rally in Southern California
item.description: Authorities say a Nevada man with a shotgun and a loaded handgun in his vehicle was arrested at a security checkpoint outside Donald Trump’s rally Saturday night in the Southern California desert.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Will Donald Trump and Kamala Harris debate again?
item.description: While there was originally talk of more than one meeting between Trump and Harris, the former president made his intentions clear with a post on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: This year's 'October surprise' may be no surprise at all
item.description: It is not uncommon for political commentators to call a late-breaking story during a presidential election an "October surprise" — even when there is little evidence that it mattered all that much.
item.dc:creator: Ron Elving, MPR News

item.title: Emily Stevens discusses accountability, safety, and fiscal responsibility on the Clark County School District Board
item.description: Emily Stevens, candidate for the Clark County School District Board of Trustees, appeared on 8 News Now's Daily Voting Guide to discuss why she believes she's the right fit for the open seat in District A.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ryan Hamilton discusses homelessness, the Animal Foundation, and F1
item.description: Ryan Hamilton, a Republican candidate for the Clark County Commission, appeared on 8 News Now's Daily Voting Guide to share his perspective on homelessness, animal welfare, and Formula One's impact on the local economy as the election nears.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News
